exemplar.title: To what extent do the promotional strategies employed by Nike Inc. in the North American region contribute to it having the greatest market share in the athletic apparel industry?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do the promotional strategies employed by Nike Inc. in the North American region contribute to it having the greatest market share in the athletic apparel industry?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic (Nikeâs promotional strategies and North American market share) is communicated accurately with clear, focused explanation.
Research question is explicitly stated, well-focused and consistently guides the analysis.
Introduction provides relevant contemporary figures and explains the investigationâs relevance effectively.
weaknesses: Methodology relies solely on secondary sources and lacks detail on search strategies and selection criteria.
Limited triangulation of data; absence of primary sources reduces methodological depth.
Minor drift occurs when analysis touches on profitability and DTC strategy without always tying back to promotions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work clearly states and explains its focus, with a sharp research question and coherent topic communication. Methodological description is purposeful but underdeveloped in demonstrating informed source selection. While the focus is maintained throughout, occasional tangents prevent a perfect score. A 5/6 reflects strong performance with room to strengthen methodological rigor.

strengths: Key concepts (e.g., âdigital marketingâ, âbrand resonanceâ, ânet profit marginâ) are defined and applied coherently.
Source material (Statista, journals, databases) is generally relevant and supports arguments.
Terminology and theoretical frameworks are used accurately and consistently for the most part.
weaknesses: Occasional inaccuracies in terminology (e.g., below-the-line vs digital marketing) and informal phrasing lower precision.
Several data points lack full citation details or critical interrogation (e.g., follower counts, campaign statistics).
Some theoretical references rely on secondary blogs rather than original academic sources.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows clear and coherent subject knowledge with mostly appropriate application of sources and generally accurate use of terminology. However, inconsistent citation detail, minor conceptual inaccuracies, and occasional reliance on non-academic sources indicate room for refinement. A 4/6 reflects solid mid-to-upper performance in Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Research draws on a good breadth of relevant secondary data (market-share figures, revenue trends, case studies).
Analysis applies the Brand Resonance Model and profitability ratios logically, offering well-supported conclusions.
Arguments are structured and linked to the research question, demonstrating clear analytical reasoning.
weaknesses: Evaluation remains partially descriptive; alternative explanations (pricing, innovation, DTC) are noted but not rigorously weighed.
Sophistication of counter-arguments is limited and some analytical connections (e.g., digital engagement to market share) need further development.
Key analytical sections (e.g., model application, ratio calculations) occasionally lack completeness and transparency in workings.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work presents good-quality research relevant to the question and clear, logically supported analysis. Critical evaluation is present but not fully developed, and deeper counter-argumentation would enhance the discussion. Awarding 8/12 recognizes strong critical thinking while acknowledging the need for more sophisticated evaluation.

strengths: The report follows a clearly appropriate EE structure, with all required sections present and logical headings.
Word count, title page, and major layout conventions (figure labels, section divisions) are correctly applied.
Overall readability is high, and structure supports the flow of the investigation.
weaknesses: Inconsistent figure numbering and caption placement detract from clarity.
Several formatting lapses (stray page numbers in body, split URLs, citation-style inconsistencies) remain.
Heading hierarchy shifts and some long text blocks reduce visual coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all core structural and formatting requirements, with only minor lapses that do not impede understanding. The work justifies the maximum 4/4 in Presentation given its clear organization and adherence to formal conventions, despite a number of correctable technical issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated in title and body, is focused (promotional strategies â contribution to market share â North America) and remains the guiding thread throughout analysis and conclusion.  Although the wording is rather broad (âto what extentâ), the study keeps a clear promotional-mix lens, so focus is maintained.  Minor drift occurs when profitability ratios and DTC strategy are examined without always tying back to promotions, preventing the full 6.

The discussion recognises limitations (lack of competitor comparison, source bias) and notes risks of negative publicity.  Evaluation is present but tends to be descriptive rather than deeply critical; alternative explanations (pricing, DTC, innovation) are acknowledged but not rigorously weighed.  This fits âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluation,â mid-band.

The student demonstrates sound understanding of promotional-mix theory, brand resonance, profitability ratios and market-share concepts, linking them to Nike.  Knowledge is coherent and usually supported by sources, though some assertions (e.g., exact causality between promotions and 5.2 % revenue growth) are taken at face value.  Depth is adequate but not consistently critical, placing it at the top of the middle band.

Key termsââdigital marketing,â âcelebrity endorsement,â âbrand resonance,â ânet profit marginââare defined and used correctly.  Occasional inaccuracies (e.g., confusion between âbelow-the-lineâ and âdigitalâ) and informal phrasing lower precision.  Consistency is good but not flawless, hence a solid mid-band score.

Analysis applies the Brand Resonance Model and profitability ratios to explore cause-and-effect.  Arguments are generally logical and supported by figures (e.g., 31 % sales boost after âDream Crazyâ).  However, quantification of âextentâ is limited and counter-arguments are brief, so sophistication is moderate, aligning with the upper part of the 4-6 band.

Data from Statista, YCharts, news articles and marketing texts are inserted to illustrate points; most graphs are referenced, but some lack full citation or critical interrogation.  Application is generally relevant but occasionally illustrative rather than analytical (e.g. revenue charts presented without ratio calculations).  This reflects âmostly relevant and appropriateâ, meriting mid-band.

Methodology relies solely on secondary sources; the student justifies this by time-zone constraints and briefly evaluates bias.  Sources include Statista, academic journals and finance databasesâgenerally relevant but not especially varied (no primary data, limited triangulation).  The selection appears purposeful yet lacks detail on how each source informs specific analytical tools, so evidence of âeffective and informedâ selection is partial rather than clear.

A fair range of secondary data (market-share figures, revenue trends, campaign case studies) is marshalled and clearly linked to the research question.  While breadth is evident, depth of primary insight is limited; nonetheless the research is unquestionably pertinent, matching descriptors for âgoodâ quality.

Word count, title and section headings are present; figures are labelled.  Page numbers are referenced in image file names but not visibly numbered in text extract; some image resolution is poor and citation formatting is inconsistent.  Most layout conventions are met but with several minor lapses, so the lower half of the top band is appropriate.

The introduction clearly states the topic (Nikeâs promotional strategies and market share in North America), explains contemporary relevance, provides recent market-share figures and describes why the issue matters to the firm.  The explanation is developed but not exhaustive: links to broader industry dynamics and alternative strategic variables (e.g. product innovation, supply chain) are only briefly noted.  Overall clarity and purpose are strong, but some contextual discussion remains surface-level, hence the lower half of the top band.

The essay follows expected EE structureâtitle page with word count, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, works cited.  Headings are logical and all core sections appear.  Minor issues (e.g., some long blocks of text without sub-subheadings) do not impede clarity, so full marks within this band are justified.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and tightly focused on Nikeâs promotional strategies and market share in North America, providing a strong foundation for the investigation.
Including a precise word count (3,995) demonstrates adherence to the 4,000-word limit requirement.
No visible page number appears in the footer; adding explicit page numbering will meet formatting conventions.
The introductionâs broad statement about health consciousness lacks a clear connection to promotional strategies; tighten the opening to align directly with your research focus.
The candidateâs personal interest anecdote, while insightful, is descriptive rather than analytical; consider relocating reflective comments to a dedicated reflection section.
The statement on how this research will benefit Nike is too general; specify actionable insights or decision-making applications.
The pie chart caption precedes the figure without in-text reference; ensure each figure is numbered consistently and referred to in the narrative.
Methodology lists syllabus areas but omits details on database searches, selection criteria, and data evaluation procedures; expand to demonstrate informed selection.
The historical background under Main Findings is descriptive and not directly tied to promotional strategies; refocus this section on findings relevant to the research question.
Market share data cited from Carlson (2024) lacks a precise source URL or page number; improve citation accuracy and critically discuss potential bias.
Figure numbering is inconsistent: Figure 1 appears again on page 5 but should be labeled Figure 2 to match the caption.
Definition of promotional strategies cites Paul Hoang (2014) without page or chapter; add precise reference details to strengthen academic rigor.
Quantitative claims (e.g., 290 million followers, 1,900 retweets) are presented without direct in-text citation or date; provide precise source references.
The discussion of digital marketing metrics should explicitly link increased engagement to changes in market share; strengthen the analytical connection.
Caption appears above the figure (Figure 3); standard academic convention places captions below figures to improve clarity.
The acknowledgement of negative feedback risk is solid; consider adding real-world data on customer complaints to deepen evaluation.
Celebrity endorsement section introduces the concept but does not quantify its impact on Nikeâs North American market share; include direct evidence.
Long blocks of text under Celebrity Endorsements reduce readability; break into sub-points or add subheadings.
The sentence on applying the Brand Resonance Model ends mid-thought; ensure the explanation and application to Nike are completed.
The Brand Resonance Model is sourced from a consultancy blog (Amati, 2022); consider citing Kellerâs original work for stronger theoretical grounding.
Your insight on high marketing costs versus brand salience is valuable; consider adding specific budget figures or ROI metrics.
The perception map (Figure 5) lacks axis labels and scale; add clear labels and a legend to enhance interpretability.
In the Brand Performance section, link the narrative directly to profitability data or ratios introduced later to improve coherence.
The heading hierarchy shifts from H2 to H3 inconsistently under Performance and Image; maintain consistent heading levels throughout.
The Dream Crazy sales boost statistic (31%) needs a reference table or chart illustrating before-and-after revenue for clarity.
A stray page number marker (â13â) appears in the body; remove it or reposition strictly in the footer.
Figure 6 references Helen (2019) for revenue data outside your study period; seek more recent campaign data to strengthen relevance.
Profitability ratios discussion refers to percentages but omits formulae and detailed calculation steps; include workings for transparency.
The timeline narrative on digital transformation (pre-2010 vs post-2010) is clear but could be supported by a tabular timeline for enhanced presentation.
Market share variances narrative is robust but would benefit from a comparative table showing year-on-year share changes in North America.
Noting direct-to-consumer impact is good, but analysis should compare the relative influence of DTC vs promotions on market share.
A page number tag (â18â) appears inline with the conclusion; remove stray numbering from the body to maintain clean formatting.
The conclusion restates findings well but could quantify the âextentâ of promotional contribution (e.g., approximate percentage) to deepen analysis.
Limitation acknowledges missing competitor comparison; in future include a competitor benchmarking section to contextualize Nikeâs performance.
Several works-cited entries lack uniform formatting for titles, italics, and access dates; apply a consistent citation style across all references.
URLs are split across lines and missing retrieval dates; ensure each URL remains unbroken and include date accessed for completeness.
The Study.com citation lacks author date consistency and lesson transcript details; verify and standardize author and date information.
DOI placement varies across entries; unify the formatting of DOIs and ensure they follow your chosen citation style.
